seo_description:
Sprint recovery, closeout technique, and immediate decision-making.
Main Goal
Train defenders to sprint into controlled closeouts after ball movement, while offensive players learn to attack or shoot decisively under pressure.
Setup
A defender starts with the ball in one corner and passes it to a passer/coach at the free-throw line.

cached_payload.title: 1v1 Corner Skip Closeout

phases.notes:
1. As soon as the pass is made, the defender sprints across to the opposite corner to prepare for a closeout.
2. The passer quickly skips the ball to that opposite corner, where an offensive player receives the pass.
phases.title: Phase 1

content.text: As soon as the pass is made, the defender sprints across to the opposite corner to prepare for a closeout.
The passer quickly skips the ball to that opposite corner, where an offensive player receives the pass.

phases.notes:
1. The defender arrives on the catch and plays a live 1v1 closeout situation, focusing on balance, angles, and recovery speed.
2. The possession continues until a shot, drive, or stop.
Optional:
Limit the offensive player to two dribbles maximum.
phases.title: Phase 2

seo_description:
Full-court 1v1 with change-of-direction read after midline touch.
Main Goal
Train change-of-pace and change-of-direction decisions for the ball-handler while developing defensive reaction speed, recovery angles, and containment in open space.
Setup
1. One ball-handler (O1) and one defender (X1) start next to each other behind the baseline. O1 has the ball.
2. At the opposite half (midcourt), two cones form a small gate on the midline. Both players must run forward and touch the midline (at least one foot on or beyond it).

cached_payload.title: 1v1 Midline Gate Decision

phases.notes: The drill starts when the ball-handler moves. The defender can react immediately and sprint as well.
phases.title: Phase 1

content.text: The drill starts when the ball-handler moves. The defender can react immediately and sprint as well.

phases.notes:
1. After touching the midline, the ball-handler can decide: continue forward and attack the far basket OR plant, change direction, and attack back to the original basket.
2. The defender must read the offensive decision, adjust angles, and defend live until the layup attempt.
3. Play until a score or defensive stop, then switch roles.
Constraints
1. Finish constraint: Offense must finish with a layup (no pull-ups or threes).
2. Dribble constraint: Ball-handler must use at least one change of direction (e.g., crossover, behind-the-back) after the midline touch.
3. Time pressure: Shot must occur within 4 seconds after the midline touch.
4. Progression: Allow defender to start half a step ahead to increase pressure and force more decisive change-of-direction moves.
phases.title: Phase 2

content.text: After touching the midline, the ball-handler can decide: continue forward and attack the far basket OR plant, change direction, and attack back to the original basket.
The defender must read the offensive decision, adjust angles, and defend live until the layup attempt.

seo_description:
Fast 2v1 that forces instant spacing + a quick, accurate pass under pressure.
Main Goal
Train quick spacing and a high-quality 'bullet pass' to beat a recovering defender, while teaching the defender to read and choose between stopping the pass or protecting the rim.
Setup
1. Three players line up in a straight line at the three-point line (same lane), one behind another.
2. Back player = ball-handler (O1). In front of him stands an off-ball attacker (O2). In front of O2 stands the defender (X1) facing the basket.

cached_payload.title: 2v1 Bullet Pass

phases.notes: 1. The rep starts when O1 begins dribbling forward. At that moment, O2 and X1 are allowed to move as well.
phases.title: Phase 1

content.text: The rep starts when O1 begins dribbling forward. At that moment, O2 and X1 are allowed to move as well.

phases.notes:
1. It becomes a live 2v1. The two offensive players must immediately create good spacing (two lanes) and attack fast.
2. The defender must read the situation and decide: deny the pass (take away the lane) or protect the rim.
3. Play until a score or stop, then rotate roles.
phases.title: Phase 2

content.text: It becomes a live 2v1. The two offensive players must immediately create good spacing (two lanes) and attack fast.
The defender must read the situation and decide: deny the pass (take away the lane) or protect the rim.
Play until a score or stop, then rotate roles.

phases.notes: Start swap: Off-ball attacker (O2) and defender (X1) switch starting positions to change angles and reads.
phases.title: Phase 3

content.text: Start swap: Off-ball attacker (O2) and defender (X1) switch starting positions to change angles and reads.

phases.notes:
Constraints
1. Mandatory pass: The ball-handler is NOT allowed to finish. He must pass for the final shot.
2. Limited dribbles: Ball-handler has max 1–2 dribbles before he must pass.
3. Finish constraint: Offense must finish with a layup (no pull-ups).
4. Initiator variation: Change who triggers the drill (e.g., O2 moves first, then O1 can dribble; or X1 moves first).
5. Passing focus: Count only 'bullet passes' (on time, on target). If the pass floats or is late, it’s an automatic stop / no point.
phases.title: Phase 4

seo_description:
Competitive shooting under pressure.
Main Goal
Force a paint touch and create a high-quality finish or kick-out against help defense.
Setup

seo_description:
Constraint-based 2v2 to train reads: reject, roll, pop, slip + different coverages.
Main Goal
Improve ball-handler and screener decision making in the on-ball screen game by manipulating coverage and finishing constraints (reject / use screen, roll / pop / slip, finish vs. help).
Setup
Set up 2 offensive players at the three-point line: a ball-handler (screen receiver) and a screener. The on-ball defender guards the ball-handler. The screener’s defender starts under the rim.

cached_payload.title: 2v2 Pick & Roll Variations

phases.notes: The screener sets an on-ball screen for the ball-handler. The ball-handler attacks immediately (initially a 2v1) and looks to score or create an advantage.
phases.title: Phase 1

content.text: The screener sets an on-ball screen for the ball-handler. The ball-handler attacks immediately (initially a 2v1) and looks to score or create an advantage.

phases.notes:
1. As soon as an offensive player enters the paint, the second defender (starting under the rim) is allowed to actively defend — turning the action into live 2v2.
2. Play until a score, stop, or defensive rebound. Reset quickly and rotate roles/positions.
Constraints
1. Coverage constraints (choose one): On-ball defender must go OVER every time / must go UNDER every time / must ICE (force sideline) every time.
2. Finish constraints (choose one): Offense can only score with layups OR threes / Offense must finish in 0–1 dribbles after the catch on the roll/pop.
3. Help defender constraints (starting under rim): cannot jump / must keep one hand behind the back / must stay inside the restricted area until paint entry / may start moving when the screen is set.
4. Offense option constraints (choose one): Ball-handler must REJECT the screen on the first rep / must USE the screen on the first rep; screener must ROLL every time / must POP every time / must SLIP every time.
5. Add a time pressure: Shot must happen within 4 seconds after the screen.
6. Progression: Start with help defender 'limited' (static/no jump), then gradually remove limitations to increase realism.

phases.notes:

cached_payload.title: Tag & Escape Warm-Up (1v1)

phases.notes:
1. The drill begins when the runner initiates movement. The runner must try to reach either cone without being tagged by the tagger’s ball.

phases.title: Phase 1

content.text: The drill begins when the runner initiates movement. The runner must try to reach either cone without being tagged by the tagger’s ball.

Constraints
1. The runner must dribble while escaping (adds complexity and ball control).
2. Vary the distance and positioning of the cones to change difficulty.
3. Move the tagger’s starting position (wider, narrower, angled).
4. The runner must perform at least one cross over (behind the back / between the legs).
